title: Dog Squinting One Eye: Causes, Urgency, and Next Steps
meta_desc: Is your dog squinting one eye shut? Learn about eye pain, urgency levels, and why you should see a vet within 24 hours to prevent vision loss.
content_html:
What Does It Mean When a Dog Squints?

When a dog squints or holds one eye tightly shut, it is medically known as blepharospasm. This involuntary contraction of the eyelid muscles is a primary indicator of ocular pain. Unlike humans, dogs cannot tell us if their eye feels itchy or painful, so squinting serves as a major red flag that the eye's surface or internal structures are irritated. Common causes include corneal ulcers (scratches), foreign objects like hair or grass seeds, or inflammation such as uveitis.

Is it an Emergency?

Squinting is classified as a Medium Urgency situation. While it may not be an immediate life-threatening emergency, eye conditions in dogs can deteriorate very quickly. A minor scratch can turn into a deep infection or a perforated eyeball within 24 to 48 hours. You should contact a veterinarian for an appointment within the same day. If the squinting is accompanied by a change in the eye's color, a bulging appearance, or sudden blindness, you should seek emergency care immediately.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a high-quality photo of your dog's eye to a veterinary professional can be incredibly helpful for triage. A clear image allows the vet to look for specific markers like the type of discharge (clear vs. yellow/green), the presence of visible debris, cloudiness of the cornea, or a difference in pupil size. Since dogs often keep the eye shut tighter when stressed at a clinic, a photo taken in a relaxed environment can offer a more natural look at the severity of the irritation.

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops on my dog?
faqs.answer: No. Never use human eye drops or medications on your dog without a veterinarian's instruction, as some ingredients can be toxic or cause a corneal ulcer to worsen rapidly.

faqs.question: What should I do if my dog is pawing at the eye?
faqs.answer: If your dog is rubbing or pawing at the eye, place an Elizabethan collar (the 'cone of shame') on them immediately to prevent self-trauma and further damage.

faqs.question: How can I tell if there is a scratch on the eye?
faqs.answer: A veterinarian must perform a fluorescein stain test, where a special dye is placed in the eye and viewed under a blue light to reveal scratches that are invisible to the naked eye.
